channel.title: FierceBiotech — Health, Longevity & Diet
channel.description: FierceBiotech articles filtered for longevity, health, diet, and healthspan research

item.title: Obesity biotech Ambrosia scores $100M series B to take next-gen oral GLP-1 into the clinic
item.description: Ambrosia Biosciences is aiming to move the market beyond peptide-based obesity treatments and initiate a trial for its oral small-molecule GLP-1 therapy, backed by an oversubscribed $100 million series B round.

item.title: Merck enlists antibody hunter Infinimmune for $838M biobucks pact
item.description: The New Jersey pharma is tapping Infinimmune to hunt for antibodies for as-of-yet undisclosed targets.

item.title: Lipella files for bankruptcy, ending dream of developing mouth inflammation drug
item.description: Lipella Pharmaceuticals’ ambition to develop the first treatment for an inflammatory mouth condition has reached the end of the road, with the biotech filing for bankruptcy.

item.title: AstraZeneca’s Strensiq successor misses phase 3 rare disease goal, denting market expansion plan
item.description: A phase 3 trial of AstraZeneca’s rare metabolic disease prospect has missed its primary endpoint, raising doubts about the company’s ability to expand beyond the children served by its existing drug.

item.title: Astellas axes early Sjögren’s trial in latest setback for autoimmune disease community
item.description: Astellas Pharma has become the latest company to pull back from a potential treatment for Sjögren’s syndrome, an autoimmune disease that still lacks any dedicated medicines.

item.title: Merck dives into detailed phase 3 data showing PCSK9 pill easily outswims rivals
item.description: Merck & Co. has lifted the lid on the third successful phase 3 trial of its PCSK9 inhibitor, showing the edge the cardiovascular disease prospect has over other oral non-statin therapies.

item.title: Kevin Tang uses newfound control of Aurinia to finally take over Kezar
item.description: Autoimmune specialist Aurinia, which named Kevin Tang as its new CEO last week, is acquiring Kezar after the latter company received promising feedback from the FDA. Tang previously made an unsuccessful attempt in 2024 to acquire Kezar via Concentra Biosciences and its parent company, Tang Capital Partners.

item.title: J&J secures $500M Royalty backing for autoimmune antibody program
item.description: Royalty Pharma has made another play in the autoimmune space, this time crowning Johnson & Johnson as the beneficiary. Royalty and J&J have agreed to co-fund the development of a chronic autoimmune disease treatment, with Royalty pitching in $500 million over the next two years.

item.title: Biogen’s litifilimab sparkles in midphase lupus trial, raising hopes for pivotal readout
item.description: Biogen’s lupus drug candidate litifilimab has improved outcomes in another phase 2 trial, adding to the momentum behind a program barreling toward pivotal data in two forms of the autoimmune disease.

item.title: Kailera plots IPO to fund obesity pipeline after one of the biggest raises of 2025
item.description: Kailera Therapeutics—one of the biggest money-raisers of 2025—is now seeking to go public as it seeks further funding to push its obesity portfolio through the clinic.

item.title: Takeda clears path to FDA with phase 3 data on $4B psoriasis bet
item.description: Takeda has posted phase 3 data on an autoimmune asset it acquired for $4 billion, laying down a marker ahead of a fight for market share with companies including Bristol Myers Squibb and Johnson & Johnson.
